element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core movement mechanic carried forward from first game
Replacement collectible for yarn balls from predecessor
New offensive capability added as feature expansion
Player agency in route selection; major departure from first game's linear progression
Improved durability vs. predecessor; reduces punishment for mistakes

element_introduction_order.element: Theme park virtual world stages (Egypt, Aztec, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Historical era-themed level variety; story context provided through Amazatorium setting

typical_relief_triggers: Nerf gun pickups enabling offensive play
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters implied by story context
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Described as boringly easy for experienced players; variable difficulty across stage paths suggests controlled pacing with relief sequences

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple paths through each stage enable player discovery of alternate routes and collectibles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible; designed with multiple path options to accommodate varying skill levels
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation through stage variety and enemy complexity across theme park worlds
notable_spikes: New features (Ballzooka gun, path selection) initially confusing according to reviews

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage offers multiple routes, allowing players to choose difficulty and exploration depth. Contrasts with first game's linearity.
Amazatorium theme park stages feature historical era designs (Egypt, Aztec, etc.), providing visual variety and narrative context for level progression
Marble pickups reward thorough exploration of alternate paths within stages, encouraging replayability
